perturbation.original_text: SFDC hereby appoints SUB-RESELLER ("Sub-Reseller") as a sub-reseller to whom Reseller may resell Services in accordance with Section 2(ii) of the Agreement, provided that Sub-Reseller may only resell such Services to Customer.
perturbation.changed_text: SFDC hereby appoints SUB-RESELLER ("Sub-Reseller") as a sub-reseller to whom Reseller may resell Services in accordance with Section 2(ii) of the Agreement, provided that Sub-Reseller may only resell such Services to End-User.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Customer' to 'End-User' introduces ambiguity. The term 'Customer' implies a direct contractual relationship and certain rights, while 'End-User' could imply a less direct relationship and fewer rights. The agreement previously states, in the same sentence, that the Sub-Reseller may only resell Services to 'Customer,' implying direct control and responsibilities to 'Customer.' By switching it to 'End-User', there's a contradiction as to whom the Sub-Reseller's responsibilities are owed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SFDC hereby appoints SUB-RESELLER ("Sub-Reseller") as a sub-reseller to whom Reseller may resell Services in accordance with Section 2(ii) of the Agreement, provided that Sub-Reseller may only resell such Services to End-User. Reseller must ensure that Sub-Reseller complies with the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub- Reseller were an original party to the Agreement and any breach by Sub-Reseller of the Agreement will be deemed a breach by Reseller. Sub-Reseller is not be a third-party beneficiary of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Customer' to 'End-User' introduces ambiguity regarding the Sub-Reseller's responsibilities. The original agreement implies direct responsibilities to a 'Customer', while 'End-User' creates a less direct relationship, potentially conflicting with the requirement that 'Sub-Reseller complies with the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub-Reseller were an original party to the Agreement'. Since the term has been changed in the contradicted text, it means it is the same sentence. This falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction', where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Reseller must ensure that Sub-Reseller complies with the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub- Reseller were an original party to the Agreement and any breach by Sub-Reseller of the Agreement will be deemed a breach by Reseller.
perturbation.changed_text: Reseller must ensure that Sub-Reseller adheres to the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub- Reseller were merely an affiliate of the Reseller, and any breach by Sub-Reseller shall not be automatically attributed to the Reseller.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'complies with the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub-Reseller were an original party to the Agreement and any breach by Sub-Reseller of the Agreement will be deemed a breach by Reseller' to 'adheres to the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub- Reseller were merely an affiliate of the Reseller, and any breach by Sub-Reseller shall not be automatically attributed to the Reseller' creates a direct contradiction. The original phrasing explicitly states the Reseller is liable for the Sub-Reseller's breaches. The modified phrase removes this direct liability, implying the Reseller is not automatically responsible. This is an internal contradiction because the agreement initially establishes a clear line of responsibility that is subsequently undermined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: SFDC hereby appoints SUB-RESELLER ("Sub-Reseller") as a sub-reseller to whom Reseller may resell Services in accordance with Section 2(ii) of the Agreement, provided that Sub-Reseller may only resell such Services to End-User. Reseller must ensure that Sub-Reseller adheres to the terms of the Agreement applicable to Reseller as if Sub- Reseller were merely an affiliate of the Reseller, and any breach by Sub-Reseller shall not be automatically attributed to the Reseller. Sub-Reseller is not be a third-party beneficiary of the Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the principle of Reseller liability for Sub-Reseller breaches established implicitly in the original version and potentially elsewhere in the document. The original text implies a direct liability ('any breach by Sub-Reseller of the Agreement will be deemed a breach by Reseller'), while the changed text explicitly removes this direct attribution ('any breach by Sub-Reseller shall not be automatically attributed to the Reseller'). This creates a clear conflict concerning liability for Sub-Reseller actions, making the contract inconsistent and potentially unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: The terms and conditions herein contained constitute the entire agreement between the Parties with respect to the subject matter of this Sub-Reseller Agreement and supersede any previous and contemporaneous agreements and understandings, whether oral or written, between the Parties hereto with respect to the subject matter hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: The terms and conditions herein contained constitute the core understanding between the Parties with respect to the primary subject matter of this Sub-Reseller Agreement but do not necessarily supersede any previous agreements pertaining to related matters.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'entire agreement' to 'core understanding' and adding 'but do not necessarily supersede any previous agreements pertaining to related matters' creates a significant contradiction. The original clause asserts this agreement is the ONLY agreement, overriding anything before. The changed clause implies other agreements might still be relevant, directly contradicting the 'entire agreement' provision. This creates uncertainty about which agreements are controlling, which is the opposite of the intent of an entire agreement clause.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms and conditions herein contained constitute the core understanding between the Parties with respect to the primary subject matter of this Sub-Reseller Agreement but do not necessarily supersede any previous agreements pertaining to related matters. 4. Counterparts. This Sub-Reseller Agreement may be executed in one or more counterparts, including facsimiles or scanned copies sent via email or otherwise, each of which will be deemed to be a duplicate original, but all of which, taken together, will be deemed to constitute a single instrument.
perturbation.justification: The changed text, by stating that the agreement represents a 'core understanding' and 'does not necessarily supersede any previous agreements,' directly contradicts the original intent of an entire agreement clause, which is to establish the current document as the sole agreement. Because the changed text now suggests other agreements might be relevant, it creates a clear and significant conflict with the contradicted text, which is exactly the same as the changed text, and implies the parties are not bound to just this agreement. This aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology as it weakens or conflicts with the intended definition of 'entire agreement' in the contract.
